closed_reason: Turn 2 Codex peer-verify reproduced C1-C8 directly. The runtime multi-streamer slice was valid as implemented; only session/root closeout metadata drift from Turn 1 was fixed in place.

contract_checkpoints.C1: PASS. sister main@d97514e, live detached d97514e, merge-base d97514e -> origin/main exit 0.
contract_checkpoints.C2: PASS. normalize_streamers() maps legacy single-streamer config to a 1-item list and preserves multi-streamer config as an N-item list.
contract_checkpoints.C3: PASS. PipelineState composite keys use {channel_id}:{video_no} when channel_id exists, keep plain video_no fallback, and get_failed_vods() returns (video_no, channel_id) tuples.
contract_checkpoints.C4: PASS. generate_reports() writes channel_id, streamer_id, platform, and thumbnail_url into metadata JSON.
contract_checkpoints.C5: PASS. run_daemon() and run_once() iterate normalize_streamers() results and keep channel_id through retry reconstruction paths.
contract_checkpoints.C6: PASS. publish builder is metadata-first for channel identity and preserves two-streamer synthetic replay without streamer collapse.
contract_checkpoints.C7: PASS. settings_ui remains importable and existing output still rebuilds as 1 VOD / 1 streamer.
contract_checkpoints.C8: PASS. Validate-DadPacket.ps1 and Validate-Documents.ps1 both passed.

decisions: Turn 1: normalize_streamers() establishes a canonical streamers list while preserving legacy target_channel_id and streamer_name compatibility.
Turn 1: PipelineState uses composite keys {channel_id}:{video_no} to avoid cross-streamer collisions while still resolving legacy plain video_no entries.
Turn 1: metadata JSON now records channel_id, streamer_id, platform, and thumbnail_url so publish can derive per-VOD identity from runtime artifacts.
Turn 1: settings_ui was left functionally backward-compatible; multi-streamer editing UX is deferred to a later slice.
Turn 2: The implementation checkpoints passed as claimed. The only correction needed was to seal the session/root state consistently with the completed runtime slice.
meta_improvements: Runtime identity should be recorded in per-VOD metadata rather than re-derived from a global single-channel config fallback.
Composite keys in PipelineState remove a whole class of cross-streamer video_no collisions without breaking existing legacy state entries.
A future slice still needs a real multi-streamer settings UI and auto-publish hook insertion, but those are no longer blockers for runtime identity correctness.
